title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)
label: baseline
task_text:
Solve this Super-Eywa grading-bank question.
Question ID: architecture-derived-B5-combinatorial-probability-random-chords
Title: B5. Combinatorial Probability (Random Chords)

Question:
Disk divided into 4 quadrants.
Add 25 random chords with endpoints in different quadrants.
What is the expected number of regions?

Grading target:
Exact numerical match.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
root_result_excerpt:
FINAL_ANSWER: 127
JUSTIFICATION: Each chord connects two different quadrants, so endpoints are in distinct quadrants. There are 4 quadrants, so possible endpoint pairs are (Q1-Q2), (Q2-Q3), (Q3-Q4), (Q4-Q1), (Q1-Q3), (Q2-Q4). The chords are random and independent. The maximum number of regions formed by n chords inside a circle is given by R = 1 + n + I, where I is the number of intersection point
